summary: Permuter-driven matching pass focused on Link's arrow item code/data and Yoshi special move edge cases. The PR had no body or human review comments, but the decomp.dev bot reported +5220 matched code bytes and +256 matched data bytes, including `itlinkarrow` `.data` reaching 100%, several `itlinkarrow` functions reaching 100%, and three Yoshi SpecialS collision functions reaching 100%. The diff is mostly codegen-shaping refactors: static inline helpers, stack/local-variable reshaping, bool/cast cleanup, data-symbol placement, and a few explicit fake/permuter helper artifacts.
key_files: src/melee/it/items/itlinkarrow.c - major Link Arrow code/data matching work, fake JObj assert file handling, static inline refactors, bool/cast cleanup
src/melee/ft/chara/ftYoshi/ftYs_SpecialS.c - permuter helper inlines and expression/stack shaping for Yoshi Egg Roll anim/coll functions
what_changed: `itlinkarrow.c` added `placeholder.h`, `baselib/forward.h`, and `baselib/gobj.h`; it also temporarily overrides `__FILE__` to `jobj.h` around fake `HSD_JObjSetRotation`/`fake_HSD_JObjSetTranslate` wrappers, then restores `itlinkarrow.c`.
Anonymous Link Arrow float tables `numbers` and `numbers_l` were replaced with address-named globals `it_803F6A78` and `it_803F6A98`; `it_803F6A78` was declared in the header.

`fn_802A81C4` changed from `void fn_802A81C4(HSD_GObj*)` to `s32 fn_802A81C4(Item_GObj*)`, switched to `GET_ITEM`/`GET_JOBJ`, and uses `(f32*) &it_803F6A28 + x9C` with `temp_r3[23]`/`temp_r3[31]` to reproduce Link Arrow random angle table access.

`it_802A850C` was heavily reshaped: early locals became `ip`, `jobj`, and `attr`; stack padding was adjusted; the owner/fighter check was nested; `ftLib_80086630(...)->mtx` feeds `HSD_MtxGetRotation`; `M_PI_2` replaced a literal; and casts to `HSD_GObj*`/`UNK_T` were removed where typed APIs allowed it.

Link Arrow animation/collision/hit logic was split into helper inlines such as `itLinkarrow_UnkMotion1_Anim_inline2`, `itLinkarrow_UnkMotion4_Coll_inline`, and `itLinkArrow_Logic98_HitShield_inline`, while many `return 0/1` sites were rewritten as `return false/true`.
`itLinkarrow_UnkMotion4_Anim` now duplicates the random-angle logic from `fn_802A81C4`; the in-code comment says it is the same function but the author could not get it to inline.

Yoshi SpecialS added local static inline permuter helpers: `perm_vel_inline`, `perm_neg_inline`, `perm_mul_inline`, and `perm_vel_x_inline`; the diff explicitly comments that some are 'permuter-generated helpers, almost certainly fake'.

Yoshi SpecialS changed expression forms around `M_PI`, `M_PI_2`, `ABS`, `gr_vel`, `self_vel.x`, stack pads, and boolean conditions, including `ABS(fp->gr_vel = fp->self_vel.x)` and a manual ternary replacement for `ABS(fp->self_vel.x)`.
Yoshi SpecialHi `ftYs_SpecialS_8012DF8C` changed `temp_f4` to a longer-lived `mag` variable and applies `mag *= getStickDirX(fp)` before computing the throw angle.
smart_moves: The data-layout fix was high leverage: putting the Link Arrow angle tables behind the state table as `it_803F6A78`/`it_803F6A98` and accessing them through `&it_803F6A28` matched the original address arithmetic and made `itlinkarrow` `.data` reach 100%.
The PR kept fake/permuter helpers local and explicitly labeled, which preserves matching progress while making future cleanup searchable.

Using small static inline boundaries instead of large semantic rewrites let the author control stack/register/call shape in sensitive functions like `itLinkArrow_Logic98_HitShield` and `itLinkarrow_UnkMotion4_Coll`.
Replacing casts and generic object accessors with typed `Item_GObj*`, `GET_ITEM`, `GET_JOBJ`, and direct `gobj->hsd_obj` reduced noise while also affecting codegen in the desired direction.

The temporary `__FILE__` override around fake JObj setters is a targeted assertion-string tactic: it localizes the hack and documents that it should go away once the neighboring `lbcoll` work is matched.

The bot results show the pass focused on near-complete functions where tiny expression changes paid off: Yoshi SpecialS loop collisions gained the last 2 bytes each, and Link Arrow functions moved from high-90s to exact matches.
decomp_lessons: For decomp matching, semantically pointless static inlines can be legitimate temporary tools when they reproduce compiler load/order behavior; mark them clearly and keep them file-local.

Data matching may depend on symbol order and base-relative indexing, not just equivalent values. Convenient standalone arrays can prevent `.data` from matching if the original code indexes through a nearby table symbol.
Stack layout is fragile: changing `PAD_STACK` sizes, using explicit `u8 pad[...]`, and moving locals into narrower scopes can be enough to close the last percent of a match.
Inlining boundaries are a first-class matching knob. If an obvious helper will not inline, duplicating the body with a comment can be preferable to leaving a function nearly matched.
Use typed gobj aliases and macros once known (`Item_GObj*`, `GET_ITEM`, `GET_JOBJ`), but verify codegen; these are both readability and matching changes.
Prefer named constants like `M_PI`, `M_PI_2`, and `deg_to_rad` over long literals when they match the compiler's constant emission and improve readability.

Header updates should accompany refined signatures and globals, but odd artifacts such as `s32 fn_802A81C4(...)` with no visible return should be tracked for later cleanup rather than assumed semantically meaningful.
naming_conventions: Matched globals use address-style names: `it_803F6A28`, `it_803F6A78`, `it_803F6A98` instead of descriptive placeholder names like `numbers`.
Item code in this PR favors `ip` for `Item*`, `attr` for `itLinkArrowAttributes*`, `jobj` for `HSD_JObj*`, and `gobj` for object handles.
Fighter code keeps established `fp` and `attributes`/`da` naming for `Fighter*` and Yoshi attribute structs.
Boolean functions should use `true`/`false` returns and comparisons where the prototype is `bool`.
`perm_*_inline` names identify local permuter/codegen artifacts, not durable semantic APIs.
Unknown item variables remain as offset names such as `xDD4_itemVar.linkarrow.x9C`; the PR does not invent field meanings.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Static inline load wrappers forced codegen around `fp->gr_vel` and `fp->self_vel.x`: `perm_vel_inline(fp)` and `perm_vel_x_inline(fp)`.
Arithmetic wrappers `perm_neg_inline` and `perm_mul_inline` changed generated instruction order without changing apparent semantics.
Manual ABS shaping was used where macro expansion did not match, e.g. ternary logic for `fp->self_vel.x` and `ABS(fp->gr_vel = fp->self_vel.x)`.
Stack and local-variable shaping used `u8 pad[4]`, `u8 pad[28]`, and `PAD_STACK(4/8/12)` in targeted functions.
Link Arrow random-angle code uses base-relative float table indexing from `(f32*) &it_803F6A28`, with offsets `temp_r3[23]` and `temp_r3[31]`.
`goto end` in `itLinkArrow_Logic98_HitShield` preserves the branch/return shape needed for the match.
`#undef __FILE__` / `#define __FILE__ "jobj.h"` around fake HSD JObj assertion helpers matches source-file metadata expected by `HSD_ASSERT`.
Some HSD object access forms were intentionally swapped (`HSD_GObjGetHSDObj` to `GET_JOBJ` or `gobj->hsd_obj`) as codegen controls.
review_feedback: No human review comments or review records were present in the slice; the PR body was empty.
decomp.dev bot reported matched code 59.36% (+0.13%, +5220 bytes) and matched data 37.72% (+0.02%, +256 bytes).

Bot reported 9 new matches, including `itlinkarrow` `.data`, `itLinkArrow_Logic98_HitShield`, `itLinkarrow_UnkMotion4_Coll`, `itLinkarrow_UnkMotion1_Coll`, `fn_802A81C4`, `itLinkArrow_Logic98_Reflected`, and three Yoshi SpecialS loop collision functions.
follow_up_queries: Search for `perm_*_inline` in Yoshi SpecialS and decide whether each fake helper can be replaced with semantic code after neighboring functions stabilize.
Revisit `fake_HSD_JObjSetRotation`, `fake_HSD_JObjSetTranslate`, and the `__FILE__` override once the noted `lbcoll` dependency is matched.
Audit `fn_802A81C4`: the prototype changed to `s32 fn_802A81C4(Item_GObj*)`, but the diff does not show an explicit return.
Compare `itLinkarrow_UnkMotion4_Anim` against `fn_802A81C4`; the source comment says it is duplicate logic because the helper would not inline.
Look for other item files where post-state-table float arrays should be promoted to address-named globals and indexed from the state table base for `.data` matching.
